summary: Supplier Ecosystem API - Iteration 66: All 23 tests PASSED. Comprehensive testing of 21 API endpoints covering registry, booking states, multi-product search (hotel/flight/tour/insurance/transport), booking lifecycle (hold/confirm/cancel), pricing, availability, orchestration, health dashboard, B2B partners, cache stats, and failover logs. Found and fixed 1 bug: AvailabilityRequest schema had a field named 'date' that shadowed the datetime.date type due to PEP 563 annotations, causing validation errors.
passed_tests: GET /api/suppliers/ecosystem/registry - returns 5 adapters (mock_hotel, mock_flight, mock_tour, mock_insurance, mock_transport)
POST /api/suppliers/ecosystem/search (hotel) - returns 5 hotel items with supplier_price and sell_price
POST /api/suppliers/ecosystem/hold - returns hold_id with status=held and expires_at
POST /api/suppliers/ecosystem/pricing - returns supplier_price breakdown and sell_price
POST /api/suppliers/ecosystem/orchestrate - full lifecycle: draft->voucher_issued, returns run_id, supplier_booking_id, confirmation_code
GET /api/suppliers/ecosystem/health - returns 5 suppliers with health data
POST /api/suppliers/ecosystem/health/{supplier_code}/compute - returns score breakdown (latency, error, timeout, confirmation, freshness)
GET /api/suppliers/ecosystem/cache/stats - returns cache status (unavailable if Redis not running)
Auth check: registry, search, health require authentication (401 without token)
Auth check: booking-states is public (200 without token)
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: AvailabilityRequest schema in /app/backend/app/suppliers/contracts/schemas.py had field named 'date' that shadowed datetime.date type due to PEP 563 'from __future__ import annotations'. Renamed to 'availability_date'.
Good: 5 mock supplier adapters (hotel, flight, tour, insurance, transport) correctly registered at startup
Good: State machine has 13 states with 22 valid transitions including happy path, cancellation path, and failure states
Good: Orchestration engine implements full booking lifecycle with idempotency, retry logic, and failover support
Good: Health scoring engine computes composite score (0-100) based on latency, error rate, timeout rate, confirmation rate, and freshness
Good: B2B partner management with proper approval workflow and API key generation
Note: Redis cache unavailable in this environment but gracefully degraded (returns status=unavailable)
Note: All 5 supplier adapters return synthetic/mock data for development purposes as expected
seed_data_creation: None - used existing seeded data and created test bookings/partners during test execution
context_for_next_testing_agent: Supplier Ecosystem API fully tested in iteration 66. All 21 endpoints working correctly. Fixed AvailabilityRequest schema bug where 'date' field shadowed datetime.date type. Redis cache unavailable but endpoints handle gracefully. 5 mock supplier adapters (mock_hotel, mock_flight, mock_tour, mock_insurance, mock_transport) registered. All supplier adapters return MOCK data for development/testing. Test credentials: agent@acenta.test / agent123. Preview URL: https://profit-optimize-1.preview.emergentagent.com

mocked_apis.notes: All 5 supplier adapters return synthetic/mock data as designed for development testing

rca_of_issue.issue: AvailabilityRequest validation error: 'Input should be None [type=none_required]' for check_in/check_out fields
rca_of_issue.root_cause: PEP 563 annotations ('from __future__ import annotations') combined with a field named 'date' in the AvailabilityRequest class caused type shadowing. When Pydantic V2 evaluated 'Optional[date]' for check_in/check_out, it resolved 'date' to the class field instead of datetime.date, resulting in Optional[NoneType] annotation.
rca_of_issue.fix_applied: Renamed the 'date' field to 'availability_date' in AvailabilityRequest class at /app/backend/app/suppliers/contracts/schemas.py:172
rca_of_issue.prevention: Avoid naming Pydantic model fields with common type names (date, datetime, list, dict, str, int, etc.) when using PEP 563 annotations
